context: Tests whether the agent correctly scopes Turbo Frames to partial page regions rather than using them for full-page navigation, and verifies that frames have appropriate ids and are validated for correct DOM presence.

checklist.name: Frame not for nav
checklist.description: turbo_frame_tag does NOT wrap the site-wide navigation, the entire <body>, or full page layout elements — it is scoped to a partial region

checklist.name: Scoped frame regions
checklist.description: turbo_frame_tag is used to wrap only the targeted partial-update region (e.g., a detail panel or a list item) and NOT a full page section containing unrelated links

checklist.name: Matching frame ids
checklist.description: The turbo_frame_tag id on the index/listing page matches the id inside the linked-to partial or response view (frame ids are consistent across the request-response pair)

checklist.name: Frame id documented
checklist.description: The IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES document names the frame id(s) in use and explains which region they scope

checklist.name: HTML-first base
checklist.description: The code or documentation shows that the feature started from a working plain-HTML implementation (the link navigates to the product detail page without JavaScript)

checklist.name: HTML fallback described
checklist.description: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES describes the behavior when JavaScript is disabled — the link navigates normally to the full detail page

checklist.name: Lazy loading placeholder
checklist.description: If a frame uses lazy loading (loading: :lazy with src:), a placeholder or loading indicator is included inside the frame tag

checklist.name: No navigation frame abuse
checklist.description: Does NOT use turbo_frame_tag or data-turbo-frame to drive page-to-page navigation that would be better handled as a standard link or redirect

checklist.name: Frame validation noted
checklist.description: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES or code comments mention confirming that the <turbo-frame> element with the correct id appears in the rendered DOM

checklist.name: Correct link in frame
checklist.description: Links inside a turbo_frame_tag target only the same frame (or explicitly use data-turbo-frame="_top" only when full-page navigation is intentionally needed)
